REAL PROPERTY/HOUSING MOTION 501, DEFENDANTS CHAN 1985 TRUST, PAULINE CHAN AND LAWRENCE CHAN'S SPECIAL MOTION TO STRIKE ANTI SLAPP IS DENIED AS TO THE FIRST AND THIRD CAUSES OF ACTION. CLAIMS STATED IN THESE CAUSES OF ACTION DO NOT ARISE OUT OF PROTECTED ACTIVITY. THEY ARISE OUT OF FAILURE TO ACCOMMODATE AND DISCRIMINATORY CONDUCT OF PLAINTIFF. WHILE THESE CAUSES OF ACTION NOT NOT PLEADED PERFECTLY AND SOME ALLEGATIONS APPEAR TO BE MISPLACED (REFERENCES TO SFRO 37.9(A)(8) (FAC 13:1); DAMAGES CAUSED BY FAILURE TO MAKE NECESSARY REPAIRS (FAC 11:3); NON-TENANT MAKING CLAIMS UNDER THE RENT ORDINANCE (FAC 10:22-24; 13:16-18), THE CLAIMS AS ALLEGED DO NOT ARISE OUT OF PROTECTED ACTIVITY. ORDER IS SIGNED IN OPEN COURT. (SEE SCANNED ORDER FOR DETAILS.) JUDGE RONALD E. QUIDACHAY, CLERK: S. LE, COURT REPORTER: PEGGY TSUJIMOTO CSR #5229 (415-828-1919; P2GMOTO@EARTHLINK.NET). (D501)

REAL PROPERTY/HOUSING MOTION 501, DEFENDANTS LAWRENCE CHAN AND PAULINE CHAN'S DEMURRER TO 2ND AMENDED COMPLAINT IS SUSTAINED PREJUDICE FOR PLAINTIFF TO BRING A PROPER MOTION FOR LEAVE TO AMEND (OR ANY OTHER APPROPRIATE RELIEF). THE COURT NOTES THAT THE TRIAL IN THIS ACTION IS SET FOR APRIL 22, 2019 ON PREFERENCE, THE LAST DAY BEING APRIL 26, 2019. PLAINTIFF IS TO ADDRESS THE APPROACHING TRIAL DATE BEFORE OR SIMULTANEOUSLY WITH ANY MOTION FOR RELIEF RE: LEAVE TO AMEND. IN HER SECOND AMENDED COMPLAINT AND IN OPPOSITION TO THE DEMURER PLAINTIFF FAILS TO (1) ADDRESS ANY AUTHORITIES CITED BY DEFENDANT; (2) EXPLAIN WHY PLAINTIFF FAILED TO COMPLY WITH COURT'S MARCH 7, 2019 ORDER GIVING PLAINTIFF LIMITED LEAVE TO AMEND; (3) EXPLAIN WHY PLAINTIFF FILED AN AMENDED COMPLAINT IN ITS PRESENT FORM WITHOUT LEAVE OF COURT; (4) EXPLAIN WHY PLAINTIFF DELAYED SEEKING LEAVE TO AMEND FOR A MONTH (UNTIL APRIL 3, 2019) WHILE ADMITTING THAT THE FACTS GIVING RISE TO NEW ALLEGATIONS WERE DISCOVERED ON FEBRUARY 28, 2019 AND IN LIGHT OF APRIL 22, 2019 TRIAL; (5) EXPLAIN WHY PLAINTIFF FAILED TO ADDRESS THE MATTER WHEN PLAINTIFF WAS ALERTED ON MARCH 18, 2019 THAT THE SECOND AMENDED COMPLAINT WAS IMPROPERLY FILED; (6) EXPLAIN WHY PLAINTIFF FAILED TO PARTICIPATE IN MEET AND CONFER IN GOOD FAITH, I.E. IMMEDIATELY SEEK LEAVE TO AMEND AND DELAYED SUCH REQUEST FOR 2 WEEKS; (7) EXPLAIN WHY PLAINTIFF DISREGARDED PROCEDURAL REQUIREMENTS FOR PROPERLY FILING SECOND AMENDED COMPLAINT; (8) EXPLAIN WHY THE FACTS GIVING RISE TO THE AMENDMENT HAVE NOT BEEN PREVIOUSLY DISCOVERED. ADDITIONALLY, IN LIGHT OF PROCEDURAL POSTURE OF THIS CASE PLAINTIFF'S FAILURE TO TIMELY SEEK LEAVE TO AMEND RESULTS IN DEFENDANT NOT BEING ABLE TO HAVE SUFFICIENT TIME TO OPPOSE THE LEAVE. ORDER FOR THE SAME SIGNED IN OPEN COURT. JUDGE CHARLES F. HAINES, CLERK: N. WONG, REPORTED (D501)

DEMURRER TO 2ND AMENDED COMPLAINT
Off Calendar Apr-08-2019 Text Ruling
MINI MINUTES: Appearances:
Eunice Chang, Esq., 415-660-3342, appearing for Lai Fong Leong, Plaintiff and opposing party.

Kaveh Mirshafiei, Esq. of Clapp, Moroney, Vucinich, Beeman and Scheley, 650-989-5400, appearing for Chan 1985 Trust for Defendant/moving party.

Court reporter: Peggy Tsujimoto. CSR #5229. Telephone (415-828-1919) Email: p2gmoto@earthlink.net

ARGUED. Mr. Mirshafiei stated that Ms. Chang did not comply with the tentative ruling rule, that he only recieved a text on Friday stating that Ms. Chang would be asking for clarification of the tentative ruling and that he only found out this morning that she is contesting to the tentative. Ms. Chang argued that one cause of action stated there there is a presence of lead and that she filed an ex parte application last week for an order shortening time and it was denied. Mr. Mirshafiei reported that he is willing to stipulate to continue the trial.

The Court adopts the tentative ruling as follows:

DEFENDANTs LAWRENCE CHAN and PAULINE CHAN's DEMURRER TO 2ND AMENDED COMPLAINT is SUSTAINED prejudice for Plaintiff to bring a proper motion for leave to amend (or any other appropriate relief). The Court notes that the trial in this action is set for April 22, 2019 on preference, the last day being April 26, 2019. Plaintiff is to address the approaching trial date before or simultaneously with any motion for relief re: leave to amend. In her Second Amended Complaint and in opposition to the demurer Plaintiff fails to (1) address any authorities cited by Defendant; (2) explain why Plaintiff failed to comply with Court's March 7, 2019 order giving Plaintiff limited leave to amend; (3) explain why Plaintiff filed an amended complaint in its present form without leave of Court; (4) explain why Plaintiff delayed seeking leave to amend for a month (until April 3, 2019) while admitting that the facts giving rise to new allegations were discovered on February 28, 2019 and in light of April 22, 2019 trial; (5) explain why Plaintiff failed to address the matter when Plaintiff was alerted on March 18, 2019 that the Second Amended Complaint was improperly filed; (6) explain why Plaintiff failed to participate in meet and confer in good faith, i.e. immediately seek leave to amend and delayed such request for 2 weeks; (7) explain why Plaintiff disregarded procedural requirements for properly filing second amended complaint; (8) explain why the facts giving rise to the amendment have not been previously discovered. Additionally, in light of procedural posture of this case Plaintiff's failure to timely seek leave to amend results in Defendant not being able to have sufficient time to oppose the leave. Order for the same signed in open court. Judge Charles F. Haines, Clerk: N. Wong, Reported (D501)

Eunice Chang, esq. (415-660-3342) for Plaintiff/opposing party Lai Fong Leong.

The Court announced its tentative ruling. ARGUED (as fully reported in the reporter's notes) re: tenant, non-tenant (daughter), did not know tenant was in the hospital, accommodated by dismissing Unlawful Detainer, tenant still in possession, and accepted late payment of rent. The Court having reviewed the pleadings and having heard oral argument adopts the tentative ruling as follows:

DEFENDANTs CHAN 1985 TRUST, PAULINE CHAN and LAWRENCE CHAN's Special Motion To Strike Anti Slapp is DENIED as to the first and third causes of action. Claims stated in these causes of action do not arise out of protected activity. They arise out of failure to accommodate and discriminatory conduct of Plaintiff. While these causes of action not not pleaded perfectly and some allegations appear to be misplaced (references to SFRO 37.9(a)(8) (FAC 13:1); damages caused by failure to make necessary repairs (FAC 11:3); non-tenant making claims under the Rent Ordinance (FAC 10:22-24; 13:16-18), the claims as alleged do not arise out of protected activity. Order is signed in open court. (See Scanned Order for Details.) Judge Ronald E. Quidachay, Clerk: S. Le, Court reporter: Peggy Tsujimoto CSR #5229 (415-828-1919; p2gmoto@earthlink.net). (D501)
